meta.title: Entertainment & On-Camera Projects | Who's to Say ? Foundation
meta.description: On-camera confidence, voice work, and media projects with defined creative scope and participant-owned outputs.

hero.title: Entertainment & On-Camera Projects
hero.subtitle: On-camera confidence, voice work, and media creation — turning performance interests into communication and storytelling projects.
hero.bestFor: People who love performing, acting, music, fandom, or being on camera — or who want to build confidence presenting.

blocks.html: Entertainment & On-Camera Projects is for people who light up in front of a microphone or camera — or want to. It focuses on on-camera confidence, voice practice, and entertainment-related creative projects in a supportive environment.

items: Practicing short intros, outros, and segments
Simple acting scenes or role-play for creative practice
Simple song introductions, playlist segments, and recording practice
Karaoke, voice recording, character voices, or podcast-style conversations
Learning how to listen back, choose favorites, and improve the next take
Discussing favorite movies, shows, musicians, or teams
Turning fandom into creative projects such as reviews, reaction videos, or top-5 lists
Practicing safe, respectful sharing with consent and review
Taking simple headshots or promotional photos
Collecting short clips into a mini reel or private portfolio
Helping families understand how to store and share materials safely
Better understanding of media and representation
Fun, affirming experiences in front of the camera
Creative materials that can be reviewed, stored, and shared safely when appropriate

getStarted.title: Get Started
getStarted.prompt: Want to explore an Entertainment Path project?

blocks.html: Sessions may be individual creator projects or small-group workshops, depending on the scope. Each engagement should have a defined purpose, deliverable, privacy plan, and consent process before public sharing.

Funding is not guaranteed. Any third-party or plan-funded purchase must be individually reviewed and approved in writing before services begin. The scope should distinguish creative production from therapy, employment placement, direct support, and general life coaching.
